title: Should concrete parameter constness adopt the compact const-default model?
summary: Resolved in favor of const-default concrete lvalue references; pointer pointee and pointer-object constness remain explicit and independent.
details: A candidate reference rule is const Foo& ro to ro: Foo& and Foo& rw to rw: mut Foo&.
The analogous pointer rule must distinguish pointer to const, const pointer object, and const pointer to const before any current spelling changes.
The real-render browser style lab compared both rules across synthetic, CUDA, ImGui, LLVM, GCC, SDL, GLFW, and Vulkan-shaped code.
The owner selected const-default references on 2026-07-11; DEC-003 records the accepted rule.

provenance.note: Owner questioned whether current concrete parameter behavior should match local-variable const-default presentation.
Current behavior is verified but the archived rationale is not accepted as a fresh design decision.

evidence.claim: Describes the current asymmetry that the question may supersede.

questions: Should const Foo& display without const?
How should pointer-to-const differ from a mutable pointer parameter?
Which unresolved style decision needs visual comparison?

last_verified.method: Owner selection captured by the real-render browser lab, then promoted to the production profile and regression suite.
